name: Curried Eggplant Strudel
recipeIngredient: 1 pound frozen phyllo dough (24 sheets), defrosted
recipeInstructions:
1. Toss the eggplant with salt and set aside for 30 minutes. Wrap in a towel and squeeze out any excess moisture.
2. In a skillet, saute onion in olive oil over medium heat until soft (about 2 minutes). Add garlic, curry powder, cumin, and chili powder, cooking an additional 2 minutes.
3. Add the reserved eggplant, tomatoes, and sugar to the skillet. Mix saffron into vinegar and add it to the mixture. Simmer for 15 minutes until eggplant is soft; add water if needed.
4. Add peanuts and let cool completely.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
6. Place a sheet of phyllo on a clean work surface, drizzling lightly with clarified butter. Layer five more sheets, adding more butter between each layer. Brush the top layer with butter.
7. Spread about a quarter of filling over the layers, mounding some along one longer side. Roll into a jelly roll shape starting at the edge with the mounded filling. Place seam-side down on a greased sheet pan and brush with additional clarified butter. Make three more strudels using remaining phyllo, filling, and butter.
8. Bake in preheated oven for 25 to 30 minutes until golden brown (moving pans front-to-back and top-to-bottom halfway through). Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
9. Cut the strudel into 1-inch-thick slices and top with yogurt.
Per hors d'oeuvre: 64 calories, 1 gm protein, 5 gm carbohydrates, 5 gm fat, 10 mg cholesterol, 2 gm saturated fat, 61 mg sodium
